prompt: This program updates a JLabel from a background thread. It sometimes shows corrupted text or freezes. What is the bug?
choices: JLabel.setText() must receive a String, not a concatenation expression
Swing components are not thread-safe — UI updates must happen on the Event Dispatch Thread via SwingUtilities.invokeLater()
new Thread() cannot be used in a Swing application — use a SwingWorker instead
frame.setVisible(true) must be called after all components are added and all threads started
explanation: Swing is single-threaded: all reads and writes to Swing components must occur on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT). Calling label.setText() from a background thread is a data race that can cause rendering corruption, hangs, or subtle bugs that only appear sometimes. Fix: wrap each update — SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> label.setText("count: " + i));
